nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Vitré
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Vetro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Rocco, Angelo, Antonio, Armonda, Vincenzo.

nameMeanings.name: Philippa
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: feminine form of Philip . In England during the Middle Ages the vernacular name Philip was borne by women as well as men, but female bearers were distinguished in Latin records by this form. It was not, however, used as a regular given name until the 19th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Philippa de Vitré was born about 1210, in France, her father, André III de VITRE, was 31 and her mother, Catherine de THOUARS, was 10. She had at least 1 son and 6 daughters with Guy VII de Montmorency Laval baron de Vitré de MONTMORENCY.

ko.lifeSummary: Philippa de Vitré(이)가 대략 1210년 프랑스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 André III de VITRE(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Catherine de THOUARS(은)는 10세였다. 그녀는 Guy VII de Montmorency Laval baron de Vitré de MONTMORENCY(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다.
